dialogues: Three ways to find 6 times 5 equals 30. You can look at the groups and count. You can skip-count: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30. Or you can add: 5 plus 5 plus 5 plus 5 plus 5 plus 5 equals 30. Same answer. All three work.
Which way made more sense to you today?
Right. Seeing the groups makes it real. You can count each one. And notice: counting those groups by 5s IS skip-counting. The picture and the counting go together.
That's right. Skip-counting is fast when you know the counts. That'll keep being useful.
You got it. Adding lets you see each group. As you add, you're also counting by 5s. That's a solid strategy too.
